svg.text: Example ways to launch and orchestrate the same modular workflow logic

g.text: Workflow orchestrators

text.tspan: Add an orchestration layer to run multiple workflows in concert

g.text: Good for pipelines and monitoring
Can dispatch script, notebook, or CLI commands
Adds execution control around the same workflow logic
Example: scheduled year-specific workflow runs with logging
Run Python script directly
Edit script variables and run file manually
Good for exploratory edits and one off runs
Example: python my_workflow.py
Run from Jupyter notebook
Edit parameters in notebook cells then execute
Good for documentation and interactive diagnostics
Example: run notebook cell or shell command
CLI hooks
Launch workflow with command line arguments
Good for repeatable terminal usage and batch scripts
Example: --year, --verbose, custom flags
Hooks map runtime options into workflow variables
JSON or YAML configuration
Store parameters outside code in config files
Good for scenario management and reproducibility
Can be used with or without CLI integration
Example: workflow reads config then runs
config.json or config.yaml
Echopop workflow script
Modular stages: ingestion, conditioning, estimation, reporting
Same core logic can be dispatched through different interfaces
